transcript:
Right now you're watching four purple wireframes — two spinning icosahedrons, two toruses — anchored in the dark, while thousands of tiny gold particles stream past leaving curved trails, like slow calligraphy accumulating over time.

Here's the detail worth catching: those purple shapes are named "never reached" in the code, and the gold particles are called "gifts." In the physics loop, when a particle drifts close to one of the shapes, the code computes a tangent vector and bends the particle's path around it instead of letting it collide. So every trail you see is built from a near-miss — motion that swerves rather than arrives. And the trail colors shift from "where gifts birth," a warm gold, into "as they fade from me," a cooling blue, recalculated for every segment, every frame.

The artist left a comment saying there's no warmth or depth in the raw numbers — that turning these trails into something resembling memory or longing happens only in your visual cortex. You're not just watching this piece. You're the only place it actually happens.
